data.narrative:
Orbem is a Series B biotech & health company founded by Pedro Gómez, Maria Laparidou, and Miguel Molina Romero, having raised €55.5M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, with private peer reference points including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach. Four same-sector, same-stage private comparables yield a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $600M (Profluent) to $3.3B (Eikon Therapeutics). The public-comp anchoring method using sector P/S multiples (6.1x median across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue data, a typical constraint for private company analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.1B to $1.5B, centered at the peer-median of $1.3B. This reflects reliance on a single applicable method across a moderately sized peer set. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the four private comparables relative to Orbem's specific technology and commercial trajectory, and the wide dispersion within the peer set ($600M to $3.3B) which signals material differences in investor perception across Series B biotech companies. Revenue disclosure would enable public-market anchoring and tighten confidence in the range.
